svg.text: CyberOS Skills — Anatomy of the Vision
Registry v0.2.0 · One connected picture · Onboarding poster for CyberSkill employees
From input source · through skill anatomy + 5 contracts · to dual-mode entry · across 4 surfaces · with self-audit + manual fine-tune loops · into multi-host distribution
① INPUT — what triggers a skill
Three trigger paths feed the same SKILL.md. The runtime detects the path; the body doesn't care.
User typing in CHAT. Supervisor classifies + routes.
② THE SKILL — folder + 5 inherited contracts
A skill is a folder with a SKILL.md. Five contracts wrap it. Three from v0.1.0; two new in v0.2.0.

g.text: v0.2.0 · self-audit truths the runtime checks

svg.text: v0.2.0 · DEC-091 · SKILL.md is the canonical source; per-host artefacts are GENERATED.
→ anthropic · mcp-tool · claude-plugin · antigravity · codex · cursor · connector
③ DUAL-MODE — one body, two front doors
Standalone (chat) or chained (envelope) — same function, same audit row, same hash chain.
no upstream envelope · supervisor reads STANDALONE_INTERVIEW.md
ONE function body · SAME logic · SAME audit row · SAME hash chain
Mode detected via pipeline_run_id + caller identity at runtime.
persona_id · skill_id · row_kind · trace_id · payload_sha256 · hash_chain
exposable_as: declares which doors the skill ships through. Build pipeline emits the right artefact per surface.
partner_connector: true ⇒ MUST also have cite_sources: required + injection_scan: required + ≥3 invariants (incl. scope-discipline + fabrication-boundary) + on_safety_change review
Skills detect their own drift at runtime, propose fixes, pause for human review, then resume from checkpoint.
SKILL.md / RUBRIC.md / INVARIANTS.md edited per the proposed_diff. skill_version PATCH bump. CHANGELOG entry with ### Driver. Pipeline resumes from the LangGraph checkpoint at the breach point.
Anomaly signals (early warning, even without invariant breach)

g.text: confidence_low_streak N of last K outputs had confidence < defer_below
user_correction_streak N "no, not that" within K turns
denylist_near_miss_streak N times the skill almost wrote denylisted content
scope_rejection_streak gateway rejected N writes for scope

svg.text: ⑥ MANUAL FINE-TUNE — the human loop
When auto-refinement can't resolve a pattern, a human takes over via the structured 7-step playbook.
⑦ HOST-ADAPTER PIPELINE — CCSM compiles to many surfaces
One source compiles to every modern agent host. Phased delivery: A done · B–E target v0.3.0/v0.4.0.

g.text: Phase A — CCSM lock ✅ DONE in v0.2.0 · 1 week. SKILL.md is source of truth; dist/ is generated, never hand-edited.
Phase B — Transpilers 🔵 v0.3.0 · 2-3 weeks. One transpiler per output target. Pure functions; embarrassingly parallel.
Phase C — Host shim 🔵 v0.3.0 · 1-2 weeks. Library every transpiled skill links against. Uniform runtime semantics.
Phase D — Equivalence test matrix 🔵 v0.3.0 · 1 week. Mechanical once shim ships.
Phase E — Partner connector pipeline 🟣 v0.4.0 · 4+ weeks. Hosted MCP + tenancy + OAuth + billing. Gated per-skill DEC.

svg.text: ⑧ DESTINATIONS — where the skill ends up
From a single SKILL.md, the same skill reaches CyberOS employees, partners, and any modern agent IDE.
P0 today: cpo + cto · P1: 7 more · P2+: rest
Same body · same hash chain · same audit row
📚 .cyberos-memory/ — the BRAIN that records what every skill did
The AGENTS.md protocol governs the BRAIN. Every skill output produces an action_log row (the runtime ledger) AND, for memory-relevant outputs, a memory file under memories/ (the BRAIN ledger).
Hash-chained. Append-mostly. Tombstoned soft-delete. Two audit streams that compose: skill activity and BRAIN evolution chain forward together as one trace_id.
Refinement records (memories/refinements/REF-NNN-...) are how skills + BRAIN learn together over time per AGENTS.md §0.4.
The complete picture, in one sentence

A skill is a folder · with a SKILL.md · wrapped by 5 inherited contracts · invokable through 2 doors · shippable to 4 surfaces · self-auditing at runtime · manually fine-tunable by humans · transpiled to N hosts · all writing to one append-only audit chain.

g.text: PRD §6 (personas) · SRS §6.1.1 (LangGraph) · SRS §6.4 (scope) · SRS §6.7 (audit) · SRS §6.12 (OBS) · DEC-027 / 029 / 048 / 050 / 052 / 055 / 061 (foundations)
DEC-090 contracts split · DEC-091 dual-mode + exposability · DEC-092 self-audit · DEC-093 manual fine-tune · CyberOS-AGENTS.md (BRAIN protocol)
